channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Trump Weighs Taiwan Arms Package After Beijing Summit
item.description: Following talks with Xi, Trump said he has not decided on a major arms package for Taiwan after hearing Chinas concerns. Xi warned that differences on Taiwan could hurtle the powers toward conflict.

item.title: CIA Director Ratcliffe Meets Cuban Officials in Havana
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe held high-level talks with Cuban officials including Raúl Castros grandson, discussing intelligence cooperation and security issues amid tensions over US energy blockade.

item.title: Kyiv Death Toll Rises to 24 From Russian Missile Strike
item.description: A Russian cruise missile flattened a Kyiv apartment building in one of the deadliest attacks on the capital in the 4-year war. Russia also reported downing 355 Ukrainian drones in a massive overnight barrage.

item.title: India and UAE sign defense and energy deals during Modi state visit
item.description: India and the UAE signed a framework for a strategic defense partnership and petroleum supply agreements during PM Modi's visit, with the UAE announcing billion in investments. The deals deepen ties as both nations navigate the Iran conflict's impact on energy markets.

item.title: Spain PM backs Eurovision boycott, says silence on Gaza "not an option"
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez defended Spain's boycott of Eurovision over Israel's participation, saying it puts the country "on the right side of history" and warning against "double standards" compared to Russia's exclusion.

item.title: Ukrainian drone pilots demonstrate edge during NATO drills on Gotland
item.description: Ukrainian drone pilots showcased advanced tactics during NATO exercises on Sweden's Gotland island, demonstrating lessons from combat experience. Meanwhile, a US House discharge petition forced a vote on nearly billion in new Ukraine aid.

item.title: Iran war deepens Lebanon's economic crisis with job losses and inflation
item.description: The Iran-Israel conflict has worsened Lebanon's economic crisis, with businesses closing, prices spiking, and job losses mounting. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has sent economic shockwaves across the Middle East.

item.title: BRICS Issues Chair Statement Amid Gulf Differences Over Iran
item.description: BRICS foreign ministers issued a chairman's statement rather than a consensus declaration after divisions emerged over Iran-related issues. The Gulf states' positions on the Iran conflict complicated usual BRICS unity.

item.title: US Admiral Says Iran's Ability to Threaten Neighbors Dramatically Degraded
item.description: A senior US naval commander stated that Iran's conventional military capabilities have been significantly diminished during the conflict, reducing Tehran's ability to threaten neighboring countries and shipping routes.

item.title: Iran Has No Trust in US, Will Negotiate Only If Serious, Araqchi Says
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi stated that Iran has no trust in US intentions and will only negotiate if Washington demonstrates seriousness. The comments came amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict.

item.title: US Scraps Deployment of 4,000 Troops to Poland
item.description: The United States cancelled plans to deploy additional troops to Poland, a significant shift in military posture amid evolving European security dynamics.

item.title: Pakistan Mediation With US in Difficult Course, Deadlock Remains Over Enriched Uranium
item.description: Pakistani mediation efforts between the US and Iran face challenges as deadlock persists over uranium enrichment issues, according to Iran's top diplomat.

item.title: UN Agencies Warn Somalia at Risk of Renewed Famine as Hunger Crisis Deepens
item.description: UN agencies issued warnings that Somalia faces renewed famine conditions as the hunger crisis deepens, with millions at risk of acute food insecurity.

item.title: Samsung Global AI Boom Spurred Looming Strike, Deep Divisions
item.description: Samsung Electronics faces industrial action as the global AI boom has intensified labor tensions and divisions within the company over working conditions and compensation.

item.title: Zelenskiy Condemns Russia After Strike on Kyiv Apartment Block Kills 24
item.description: President Zelenskiy condemned Russia after a missile strike on a Kyiv residential building killed 24 people, one of the deadliest attacks on the Ukrainian capital in the war.

item.title: Germany Arrests Ukrainian National Suspected of Spying for Russia
item.description: German authorities arrested a Ukrainian national on suspicion of espionage for Russia, highlighting continued intelligence threats in Europe.

item.title: Japan's Q1 GDP Likely Rose on Firm Exports
item.description: Japan's first quarter GDP is expected to show growth driven by strong export performance, according to Reuters economic forecasts.

item.title: Gazprom's Astrakhan Gas Plant Halted Motor Fuel Output After May 13 Drone Attack
item.description: A drone attack on May 13 forced Gazprom's Astrakhan gas processing plant to halt motor fuel production, highlighting continued Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: Iraqi Parliament Approves Partial Government Led by PM Zaidi
item.description: Iraq's parliament approved a partial government led by Prime Minister Zaidi, moving toward forming a complete cabinet after prolonged political negotiations.

item.title: Explosions Heard During Bolivia March by Mining Groups Calling for Resignation
item.description: Explosions were reported during protests by mining groups in Bolivia demanding government resignations amid ongoing political tensions.

item.title: AP Reports: Iran War Death Toll Rises as US and Iran Trade Strikes
item.description: The Associated Press reports on mounting casualties in the Iran conflict as US and Iranian forces continue to exchange attacks, with concerns growing about regional escalation.

item.title: Al Jazeera: Iranian Diplomat Araqchi Doubts US Seriousness on Nuclear Talks
item.description: Iran's top diplomat expressed skepticism about US intentions in nuclear negotiations, citing a lack of trust and demanding concrete demonstrations of seriousness from Washington.

item.title: Russia Pounds Ukraine in Heaviest Wartime Drone Attack Over Two Days
item.description: Russia launched its largest drone assault of the war over a two-day period, hitting Kyiv and other cities with hundreds of drones. The attack marked a major escalation following the expiration of a brief ceasefire.

item.title: Ukraine Hits Russia's Energy Targets After US-Brokered Ceasefire Ends
item.description: Ukrainian forces resumed drone attacks on Russian oil refineries and ports following the conclusion of a three-day ceasefire proposed by the US, targeting critical energy infrastructure.

item.title: Russia Launches Huge Drone Attack on Western Ukraine by NATO Border, Killing Six
item.description: Russia unleashed a massive daytime drone attack on western Ukraine near the NATO border, killing at least six people and prompting Poland to scramble fighter jets in response.
